summary: Exact-match SysDolphin decomp slice touching four baselib files. The PR body claims 2 newly matched functions with clean runner validation; the bot report names AXDriver_8038E498 and fn_80393C14 as new matches, plus score improvements in sislib and cobj. Review discussion specifically flags particle.c changes: a prior static data change was called out as a regression, and newly added pragmas were noted as recommended against.

key_files.role: Newly matched function AXDriver_8038E498; changed loop body from pointer-walk temporary to direct indexed access of AXDriver_804C45A0.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists AXDriver_8038E498 as 100.00% new match

key_files.role: Small score improvement in HSD_CObjGetLeftVector; removed a temporary result variable and called HSD_CObjGetUpVector directly in the condition.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists HSD_CObjGetLeftVector improvement from 98.65% to 98.74%

key_files.role: Newly matched function fn_80393C14; final diff adds a tightly scoped #pragma push / #pragma pool_data off / #pragma pop around the function. Review comments flag particle changes as risky/regressive and note new pragmas are recommended against.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists fn_80393C14 as 100.00% new match
review_comments.md: PsiLupan regression warning on particle.c
review_comments.md: fjooord comment that new pragmas are recommended against

key_files.role: Unmatched-function score improvements across HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C, and HSD_SisLib_803A84BC via stack buffer sizing, initialization, declaration order, typed string_buffer access, and restoring calls to HSD_SisLib_803A8134.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C, HSD_SisLib_803A84BC hunks
text_corpus: bot report lists sislib score improvements

what_changed: PR body describes an exact-match decompilation slice for 4 files with 2 newly matched functions and clean regression reports.
AXDriver_8038E498 changed a loop from an HSD_SM* pointer cursor to direct indexed access on AXDriver_804C45A0[i].
HSD_CObjGetLeftVector removed an intermediate int res and directly tested HSD_CObjGetUpVector(cobj, &up) == 0.
fn_80393C14 in particle.c was wrapped with #pragma push, #pragma pool_data off, and #pragma pop.
HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 and HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 changed local buffer sizes from 256 to 128; HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 also initializes encoded[0] = 0.
HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C moved flag_hi declaration earlier and replaced repeated ((u8*) text->string_buffer) casts with direct text->string_buffer indexing or pointer casts from text->string_buffer.
HSD_SisLib_803A84BC replaced two commented-out HSD_SisLib_803A8134 markers with actual calls passing sis_cursor, text, and output width/height pointers.

smart_moves.text: Using direct indexed array access in AXDriver_8038E498 kept the loop simple while still reaching a reported exact match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new match for AXDriver_8038E498

smart_moves.text: Replacing raw casts on text->string_buffer with direct member indexing in HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C improved readability without losing the reported score improvement.
Restoring real HSD_SisLib_803A8134 calls in place of commented placeholders made HSD_SisLib_803A84BC more source-like and correlated with the largest reported unmatched-item improvement in the slice.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report HSD_SisLib_803A84BC +45 bytes improvement

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small C-shape changes such as direct condition calls, declaration order, local buffer sizes, and direct typed/member indexing can move unmatched SysDolphin functions closer without large rewrites.
Pragma-based matching, even when tightly scoped, is review-sensitive; this PR's particle.c pragma addition drew a comment that new pragmas are recommended against.
Data/storage changes in particle.c require extra scrutiny: review feedback states a submitted change had previously been called out as a regression.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: diff hunk showing static char lbl_8040A540[0x268]

naming_conventions.convention: This slice preserves existing address-style and subsystem names such as AXDriver_8038E498, fn_80393C14, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C, and lbl_8040A540 rather than introducing speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: function names in changed hunks

naming_conventions.convention: Short local role names remain in use where already present, including buf, size, pos, entry, flag_hi, line_width_out, and line_height_out.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Pointer cursor versus array indexing can be a meaningful MWCC/codegen lever; AXDriver_8038E498 matched after replacing a manual HSD_SM* increment with AXDriver_804C45A0[i] accesses.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report AXDriver_8038E498 new match

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Local stack layout was adjusted in sislib by reducing u8 buffer[256] to u8 buffer[128] in two varargs formatting functions; this is supported only as a PR-specific matching observation, not a general sizing rule.
Declaration order and temporary elimination were used as narrow matching tweaks: cobj removes res; sislib moves flag_hi before other s32 locals.

#pragma pool_data off was used around fn_80393C14 and is associated with a reported exact match, but review feedback says new pragmas are recommended against, so this should be treated as an exception requiring strong justification.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report fn_80393C14 new match

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: A particle.c change had been submitted before and called out as a regression; the reviewer asked for closer PR review to avoid repeat issues.

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.feedback: New pragmas were noted as recommended against, referencing Discord discussion.

follow_up_queries: Search later or adjacent PRs for fn_80393C14 and #pragma pool_data off to see whether the pragma remained or was removed.
Search particle.c history for lbl_8040A540 to identify the regression referenced in review and whether it was reverted before merge.
Search SysDolphin sislib PRs for HSD_SisLib_803A8134 call recovery patterns in HSD_SisLib_803A84BC.
Search axdriver matching notes for AXDriver_804C45A0 indexed access versus pointer cursor loops.
Search project review comments for repeated guidance on new pragmas and pool_data pragmas.

evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: PR body with files and verification
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report with match/improvement table
raw_diff: full diff for final changed files
notes: PR body and bot report provide strong evidence for claimed match/improvement outcomes in this slice.

Review comment about static char lbl_8040A540 is present, but the loaded final raw diff only shows particle.c pragma additions; the static data hunk may have existed in an earlier revision not included in the final diff excerpt.
The PR body claims clean regression reports, but no raw build logs or objdiff artifacts are loaded beyond the PR body and bot comment.
Reviewer intent beyond the explicit particle.c comments is not inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: SysDolphin matching used narrow C-shape tweaks across axdriver, cobj, and sislib
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2659, exact or improved matches came from narrow source-shape edits: indexed array access instead of pointer cursor in AXDriver_8038E498, direct condition call in HSD_CObjGetLeftVector, stack buffer sizing and initialization in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98/HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, declaration order in HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C, and real HSD_SisLib_803A8134 calls in HSD_SisLib_803A84BC.

accepted_candidate_records.title: particle.c pragma/data changes are review-risky
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2659 review feedback on particle.c warned about a repeated regression and explicitly noted that new pragmas are recommended against. Treat particle.c pragma or data-storage matching changes as requiring close review and evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Pragma exceptions should cite review and regression evidence
source_update_candidates.text: This PR reinforces the existing standard that new pragmas are discouraged and should be exception-only. The loaded review explicitly says the particle.c pragmas are recommended against even though fn_80393C14 was reported as a new match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report fn_80393C14 new match
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential reinforcement/example for avoid-pragmas-register-asm and matching-tactics-need-evidence; do not promote as a new standard without curator review because the existing standards already cover this.

source_update_candidates.title: Repeated data-regression comments should be tracked for particle.c
source_update_candidates.text: Review feedback says a particle.c change had been submitted before and called out as a regression. Curator may want to link this PR as an example under data/literal ownership or regression-ledger guidance after checking the earlier PR/history.
source_update_candidates.reason: The final diff in the loaded slice does not include the static char change, so this is only a follow-up candidate, not an accepted fact about the final merged diff.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize buffer[128] as a sislib standard; evidence only shows two local matching edits in this PR.
Do not mark #pragma pool_data off as accepted style; the loaded review says new pragmas are recommended against.
Do not infer exact functions newly matched beyond AXDriver_8038E498 and fn_80393C14; those are the only ones named in the bot new-match table.
Do not claim the static char lbl_8040A540 landed in the final diff; it appears only in a review hunk, not the loaded raw final diff.
